title: Narrative Arc — Self-Healing Systems Need Governance
recognition: Self-healing systems promise shorter incidents and less repetitive operational work.
reframe: Automated repair is an authority model because the system can change production conditions on its own.
framework: Safe automatic actions, approval-required actions, forbidden actions, remediation signals, escalation, stop conditions, rollback, and observability define the governance model.
proof: The draft grounds the argument in concrete remediation categories while withholding unverified technical and quantitative claims.
application: Platform and SRE leaders can match remediation authority to consequence and preserve evidence for review.
consequence: Governed self-healing can scale responsibility without turning automation into opaque operational uncertainty.
invitation: Design when the system must not repair itself, not only how it can.
